danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental obstacles integrated into level design
danger_signals.meaning: Lemmings will die or be blocked if they encounter these without protective skills
Failure state approaching; lemmings must reach exit before time expires

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual feedback when lemmings contact traps
danger_signals.meaning: Active danger; lemming death or removal from viable solution state

safety_signals.meaning: Safe destination; delivering lemmings here completes level objective

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual representation of assigned skills (Builder creating terrain, Blocker stopping movement)
safety_signals.meaning: Lemmings are protected from specific hazard classes

safety_signals.visual_form: Terrain modifications created by Builder/Planter skills
safety_signals.meaning: Safe route established for lemming passage

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visual appearance matches original Lemmings game; behavioral differences (preserves Blocker skill, original behavioral quirks)

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Lemming Island under prophecy of darkness
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Twelve distinct tribal worlds with thematic visual differentiation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Twelve tribes organized by region; each tribe must be guided to collect talisman piece

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Each of twelve worlds represents one talisman piece location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must complete all ten levels per world with gold medal ranking to secure talisman piece

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Medal ranking system (gold/silver/bronze) displayed after level completion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Gold medal required for world advancement; lower medals unlock next level but not world completion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game provides 'nuke' option to reset level
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can restart when lemming population becomes insufficient to solve level

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of twelve worlds visually represents distinct lemming culture (Highland, Cavelem, Circus, etc.) through environmental design rather than mechanical differences
Talisman collection across twelve tribes communicates overall quest to save Lemming Island from prophesied darkness
While all skills are mechanically available, certain skills appear more frequently in specific tribal worlds, suggesting cultural preferences without enforcing mechanical exclusivity
Gold/silver/bronze medals communicate skill mastery and world completion status visually

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Walks forward automatically; enters level and seeks exit independently
Performs skill function: building terrain, mining, using mobility enhancements, affecting other lemmings' behavior
Reverses direction; cannot pass Blocker position
Propels lemmings with relevant skills (Ballooner, Surfer) and powers level mechanisms based on proximity and positioning

notes: Lemmings 2: The Tribes uses visual and mechanical differentiation across twelve tribal worlds to communicate narrative progression (talisman collection) without creating exclusive faction mechanics. Danger signals derive from environmental traps and time pressure. Safety signals emerge through skill assignment and exit location visibility. The medal ranking system (gold/silver/bronze) provides clear visual feedback on world completion status. The 'nuke' reset function communicates game state recovery option when puzzle becomes unsolvable. SNES port suffered technical limitations affecting feature completeness compared to other platforms.
